translator: Swami Adidevananda (per Sri Ramakrishna Math edition; bhagavad-gita.us reproduces this English rendering of Sri Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya)
verbatim_excerpt: 2.47 As for obligatory, occasional and desiderative acts taught in the Vedas and associated with some result or other, you, an aspirant established in Sattva, have the right only to perform them: You have no right to the fruits known to be derived from such acts.
copyright_holder: Sri Ramakrishna Math, Chennai (publisher of Swami Adidevananda's English translation of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya). Site bhagavad-gita.us reproduces the translation; we cite a short fair-use excerpt only.
notes: Same page as Shankara — static HTML contains all sampradaya commentaries, JS only toggles visibility. Full commentary length 1306 chars; excerpt is the opening 263 chars covering Ramanuja's signature framing: the verse addresses an aspirant established in sattva who has the right to Vedic-prescribed acts (obligatory/occasional/desiderative) but not to their fruits. The site's text contains a typo 'desrious' for 'desirous' that we did NOT include in the excerpt.
